notes: This batch treats run_stage16_sub4_axis6u.py as a standalone absolute Stage16 baseline surface.
The mixed-axis6 family, the Stage16 V4/V5 descendants, and the adjacent terrain8 sign suite were read only as comparison anchors.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Catalogs the current Stage16 baseline surface, the Stage16 V4/V5 descendants, and the adjacent terrain8 sign suite.
Contains the V4/V5 Stage16 descendant blocks but no S_SIM_STAGE16_SUB4_AXIS6U block.
Provides the mixed-axis6 family whose U_* baseline matches the current absolute surface up to floating-point noise.
Exposes the U_Smean and U_Pmean baseline fields for cellwise comparison.
Confirms the current batch is the Stage16 baseline anchor rather than a mixed-axis6 comparison surface.
Comparison anchor for the older top-level Stage16 delta descendant.
Comparison anchor for the newer top-level Stage16 delta descendant; byte-identical to V4.
Boundary-check anchor showing that the next raw-order file starts a terrain-only family.
Boundary-check anchor showing the adjacent family has its own paired result surface and sign-by-terrain metric contract.

runtime_expectations: run_stage16_sub4_axis6u.py writes one results_stage16_sub4_axis6u.json and one local evidence block for S_SIM_STAGE16_SUB4_AXIS6U.
The runner uses 512 random one-qubit states, the Se/Ne/Ni/Si terrain order, fixed Type1/Type2 outer and inner axis6 patterns, and one four-operator stage stack.
The adjacent run_terrain8_sign_suite.py uses a different contract: 256 states, 64 cycles, no Stage16 outer/inner split, and sign-by-terrain metrics only.
failure_modes: The current absolute surface can be mistaken for a repo-top evidenced Stage16 artifact because related V4/V5 descendants are present in the evidence pack.
The current result can be mistaken for the same payload as the V4/V5 descendants even though the contracts and output hashes differ.
The adjacent terrain8 sign suite can be merged incorrectly into this batch because of raw-folder adjacency alone.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
